title: Ginger Korean Cauliflower Bowl
description: A peppery, fluffy bowl of roasted cauliflower, seared chicken, and a tangy Korean glaze, perfect for a lazy Sunday Whole30-friendly meal.
instructions: Cut the cauliflower into bite‑sized florets and toss them with 1 tbsp sesame oil, salt, and pepper.
Roast the cauliflower for 20 minutes, stirring halfway, until golden and tender.
While the cauliflower roasts, season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, and 1 tsp gochugaru, then sear it in a hot skillet with 1 tsp sesame oil until cooked through, about 6 minutes per side.
In the same skillet, add minced garlic and ginger and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in tamari, lime juice, and a splash of water, then simmer for 2 minutes to create a glaze.
Slice the cooked chicken and combine it with the roasted cauliflower, green onions, sesame seeds, and the glaze; toss to coat evenly.
Serve immediately, optionally garnishing with extra sesame seeds and green onion slices.
